name: Stanford University
overall_acceptance_rate: Not released for 2026 cycle; latest Class of 2028: 3.61% (2,067/57,326)
english_test_requirements: TOEFL, IELTS, or Duolingo for non-native speakers (assumed standard; not explicitly for 2026)
application_pathways_eligibility: First-year: REA (Nov 1), RD (Jan 5); Transfer: separate app Mar 15; All via Common App
deadline_calendar: REA: Nov 1 (notif mid-Dec); RD: Jan 5 (notif early Apr); Arts Portfolio: Dec 5; Financial Aid priority: Nov 15 (REA)
program_supplemental_requirements: Stanford Questions: 3 short essays (100-250 words), 3 short answers (50 words); Roommate note; No major-specific
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfer app deadline Mar 15; Deferred entry (gap year) available upon request post-admit
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Not released; admits to university undeclared
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Not released; private university, no in-state preference
admitted_sat_act_percentiles: Class 2028 submitters: SAT m50% 1510-1570, ACT 34-35; Required for 2030
need_policy: Need-blind for US citizens/PR/undocumented US HS; Need-aware for internationals
class_profile_composition: Class 2029 enrolled: 1,866; 20% first-gen; 58% public HS, 28% private, 14% int; Ethnic: Asian 35%, Black 4%, Hisp 12%, White 25%, Multi 10%, Int 14%; Gender 48%W/52%M; 50 states/64 countries
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Need-based only; Priority Nov 15 (REA); Meets full need, no loans; No merit
major_impaction_signals: No signals; undeclared admission, change major open
average_aid_merit_statistics: Avg need-based grant $74,310 (freshman class); No merit
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Small % deferred from REA; Waitlist admits rare (~6% recent)

name: University of Oxford
test_policy: Subject-specific admissions tests required for ~50 courses (e.g., LNAT Law, MAT Maths/Physics, ESAT Biomedical); online Oct 2025 for 2026 entry; no SAT/ACT
english_test_requirements: IELTS Academic 7.5 overall (≥7.0 each); TOEFL iBT 110 (≥22 L/R, ≥24 S/W); other equiv.; higher level required for all courses
application_pathways_eligibility: All via UCAS (choose college or open); same process UK/int'l/mature; no quotas except Medicine int'l cap; second-degree applicants welcome
deadline_calendar: UCAS: 15 Oct 2025 6pm UK; Tests: 21-27 Oct; Written work: 10 Nov; Interviews: early-mid Dec; Decisions: 13-14 Jan 2026. Single deadline, no ED/EA/RD
program_supplemental_requirements: Course-specific: admissions tests (~50 courses), written work (humanities/languages), portfolio (Art); see table per course
transfer_deferred_entry_options: No transfers (start year 1); Deferred entry accepted except Fine Art, History of Art, Grad Medicine; competitive
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies widely: Economics & Mgmt 5.5%, Comp Sci 6.4%, Maths & Phil 6.9%, Biomed Sci 8.7%, Medicine ~9% (2024/25)
need_policy: Need-aware, esp. international; aid assessed post-admission via household income
class_profile_composition: 3,245 admitted (2024); 80.5% UK; 85.6% A*AA+ A-levels; gender/ethnicity/SES breakdowns in report
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Crankstart (UK ≤£32,500 income, auto); Reach Oxford (low-income countries, app 4 Feb 2026); college-specific
major_impaction_signals: Economics & Mgmt, Comp Sci, Maths most competitive (<7% rates); Medicine capped for int'l
average_aid_merit_statistics: Oxford Bursary: up to £6,270/yr for lowest-income UK; limited merit/need for int'l
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Deferred entry competitive (strongest applicants); ~10% pooled/reallocated via Winter Pool

name: University of Cambridge
overall_acceptance_rate: 16.4% (3,632 acceptances out of 22,153 applications in 2024 admissions cycle)
test_policy: No SAT/ACT required. Course-specific pre-interview admissions tests required for many programs (e.g. ESAT for Engineering, Natural Sciences; TMUA for Computer Science, Economics; LNAT for Law; UCAT for Medicine). College-specific at-interview assessments for some arts/humanities.
english_test_requirements: IELTS Academic: 7.5 overall (7.0+ each); TOEFL iBT/Home (pre-Jan 2026): 110 overall (25+ each); Cambridge C2 Proficiency: 200 (185+ no element); or equivalent C1 CEFR from school exam.
application_pathways_eligibility: All undergraduate applicants apply via UCAS. Same process for UK/Home, EU, International, mature (21+), disabled, care leavers. Mature may have January UCAS deadline but Cambridge requires October deadline for consideration.
deadline_calendar: Single deadline: UCAS application by 15 October 2026 (6pm UK time) for 2027 entry. My Cambridge Application by 22 October 2026. No ED/EA/RD rounds.
program_supplemental_requirements: My Cambridge Application (post-UCAS): funding evidence for international students, optional Cambridge-specific personal statement, £60 application fee for overseas applicants. Written work submission for some courses (e.g. History, English).
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Deferred entry: possible, specify in UCAS. Transfers: possible from other UK universities but rare and course/college-specific; not from overseas. Check course pages.
program_specific_acceptance_rate: Varies: Architecture 12.2% (64/526), Veterinary Medicine 19.9% (67/336), Natural Sciences ~14%; full table in 2024 cycle report (22k apps total).
acceptance_rate_by_residency: Not explicitly stated; Home (UK) schools: Maintained 18.8%, Independent 11.1%; Overseas implied lower (~10-12% from secondary sources). No in-state/OOS (UK national).
need_policy: Not explicitly stated; likely need-aware for international students (must provide funding evidence); Home students government loans.
class_profile_composition: 48% men:52% women; 15.8% declared disability; 94.7% A*AA or better (best 3 A-levels); from maintained schools 52.2% of acceptances.
tuition_fees: Home (first UG): £9,535/year; International: £29,052 (Group 1 arts/humanities) to £50,000+ (Medicine/Vet) + College fee £10,017-£13,662 (2025-26 rates, fixed for course duration).
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Cambridge Trust (int'l, post-offer); college bursaries; eligibility: academic merit/need; deadlines vary (often Jan-May post-offer). Full funding evidence required by May/June for visa.
major_impaction_signals: High competition: Medicine, Computer Science, Economics, HSPS (success rates <15%); ~6 apps/place overall.
deferral_waitlist_indicators: Winter Pool: ~1,000 applicants pooled, ~200 offers (~20% success); August pool for unmet conditions.

name: ETH Zurich
overall_acceptance_rate: No official rate published; third-party estimates ~27% overall (Bachelor's 25-30%, Master's 10-20%)
test_policy: ETH entrance examination required for many international Bachelor's applicants without Swiss Matura equivalent (comprehensive 8 subjects or reduced 4 subjects); no SAT/ACT
english_test_requirements: Not required for Bachelor's (German C1 required); Master's: TOEFL iBT 100, IELTS 7.0, or equivalent (exemptions apply)
application_pathways_eligibility: Bachelor's: Swiss Matura direct; non-Swiss: swissuniversities qualification or ETH entrance exam; Master's: Bachelor's degree matching program requirements
deadline_calendar: Bachelor's Autumn 2026: 1 Dec 2025 - 31 Mar 2026; Master's Autumn 2026: Internationals 1-30 Nov 2025, Swiss Bachelor's 1-30 Apr 2026; no ED/EA
program_supplemental_requirements: No essays, LORs, or supplements; standard docs: transcripts, language cert, declaration; program choice in app
transfer_deferred_entry_options: Transfer: Recognition up to 60 ECTS from other universities (fee CHF 50); Deferred: Possible for military/civil service; no general deferral
need_policy: Need-aware; scholarships consider family income/assets after parental responsibility
class_profile_composition: New Bachelor's: ~35% women, ~27% international; total students 26k, 43% intl
selectivity_trend: New Bachelor's enrollments stable ~3600/year after growth; total students up from 21k to 26k
tuition_fees: CHF 730/semester (Swiss residents); CHF 2190/semester (intl from HS2025); + semester fees ~74CHF
scholarship_deadlines_eligibility: Social scholarships: Sep 1-Oct 31 for next year; ESOP with Master's app Nov; need-based/merit, first-time students, progress req
major_impaction_signals: Mechanical Engineering and Computer Science most popular Bachelor's programs
